variables.name: PUBSUB_REDIS_CHANNEL_TYPE
variables.description:
Event transport type. Options are:

 - pubsub: normal Pub/Sub (at-most-once)
 - sharded: sharded Pub/Sub (at-most-once)
 - streams: Redis Streams (at-least-once, recommended to avoid subscriber races)

Note: Before enabling 'streams' in production, estimate your expected event volume and retention needs.
Configure Redis memory limits and stream trimming appropriately (e.g., MAXLEN and key expiry) to reduce
the risk of data loss from Redis auto-eviction under memory pressure.
Also accepts ENV: EVENT_BUS_REDIS_CHANNEL_TYPE.

variables.name: PUBSUB_REDIS_URL
variables.description: Redis connection URL for streaming events between API and celery worker; defaults to URL constructed from `REDIS_*` configurations. Also accepts ENV: EVENT_BUS_REDIS_URL.

variables.name: PUBSUB_REDIS_USE_CLUSTERS
variables.description: Enable Redis Cluster mode for pub/sub or streams transport. Recommended for large deployments. Also accepts ENV: EVENT_BUS_REDIS_USE_CLUSTERS.

variables.name: PUBSUB_STREAMS_RETENTION_SECONDS
variables.description: When using 'streams', expire each stream key this many seconds after the last event is published. Also accepts ENV: EVENT_BUS_STREAMS_RETENTION_SECONDS.
